memory.summary: With free time and low stress, I could relax or do a quick chore, but my term project is critically behind schedule.
I spent 30 minutes on a trivial chore, gaining minimal progress while my term project remains critically behind schedule.
Spending 30 minutes on a trivial chore yielded minimal progress while my term project remains critically behind schedule.
Spending 1.5 hours on trivial chores is wasting time while my project remains critically behind schedule and the afternoon slips away.
I spent hours on trivial chores while my term project remains critically behind schedule, wasting time that could have reduced future stress.
Spending time on trivial chores yields minimal progress while my term project remains critically behind schedule, wasting time that could reduce future stress.
I am stuck in a loop of low-value chores that provide instant wins but leave my critical project behind, despite knowing this is inefficient.
I completed a trivial chore for instant satisfaction, but this reinforces my pattern of neglecting the critically behind-term project while stress remains high.
Working on trivial chores still yields minimal progress while my term project remains critically behind schedule, reinforcing an inefficient pattern that fails to reduce stress.
I spent 1.5 hours on a trivial chore that only advanced my progress by 0.05, yet the task remains unfinished despite being due in under an hour.
I missed the deadline for a trivial chore by spending time on it, confirming that such tasks waste energy without resolving my critical project backlog.
Spending more time on trivial chores yields negligible progress while my critical project remains dangerously behind schedule, confirming this pattern is counterproductive.

goals.description: Complete the trivial easy chore to clear immediate obligations and gain momentum.
goals.rationale: The task has a deadline of Mon 22:00 (imminent) and is marked as 'soft' hardness with low progress. Completing it first removes a small but urgent item from the list.

goals.description: Work on the term project for at least 30 minutes to increase progress before Thursday.
goals.rationale: The project is due Thu 23:59 with only ~10% progress. While less urgent than the chore, it requires dedicated time to avoid falling behind significantly in the next three days.

agent.last_reasoning: The chore deadline (Mon 22:00) is critically imminent while I am already past it. Completing this 'instant win' removes a high-priority stressor immediately, preventing further accumulation of stress before tackling the term project.
